full_text:
IMD issues heat stroke alert in MP, UP:Temp crosses 40°C; storm-rain likely in R’than, yellow rain alert in Uttarakhand
- Copy link
The effect of heat is continuously increasing in Madhya Pradesh. Due to this, the risk of heat stroke has increased. On Friday, the temperature in Narmadapuram remained above 40C for the second consecutive day.
According to the Meteorological Department, the weather in the state may change again from March 15. During this time, there may be rain in some areas.
Meanwhile, the effect of intense heat has started showing in Uttar Pradesh. On Friday, the daytime temperature in the state remained above 40C for the first time. Banda's temperature was 40.2C. Besides this, the temperature was 38.3C in Jhansi and 37.8C in Orai. Today also, fog persisted in 10 districts including Gorakhpur in the morning.
Clouds covered several cities in Rajasthan on Friday. This caused a drop in temperature. The mercury dropped by 2C to 4C in many cities including Churu and Alwar. According to the Meteorological Department, there is a yellow alert for clouds, dusty winds, and rain in 5 districts today. There is a possibility of thunderstorms and rain in 18 districts on March 15.
There is a possibility of rain and snowfall in 8 districts of Uttarakhand today. According to the weather department, there is a yellow alert for rain in 5 districts including Uttarkashi and Rudraprayag today. The maximum temperature in the state is likely to be around 31C and minimum temperature around 17C.
Weather forecast for next 2 days...
March 15 - Most places in Delhi will record an increase in temperature. Rain warning has been issued for Madhya Pradesh, Jammu-Kashmir, Ladakh, Punjab, Haryana and northeastern states.
March 16 - Thunder and lightning alert in Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Sikkim. Rain is likely in Maharashtra.
